name: Twenty One Pilots
description:
'''Twenty One Pilots'''{{efn|Stylized in [[all lowercase]] as '''twenty one pilots''', '''twenty øne piløts''' or '''twenty {{!}} one {{!}} pilots''', and abbreviated similarly ('''tøp''' or '''TØP''')<ref name="AMbio"/>}} is an American musical duo from [[Columbus, Ohio]] which consists of [[Tyler Joseph]] and [[Josh Dun]]. The band is known for its difficult to categorise mix of [[musical styles]], lyrics concerning personal insecurities, and ambitious [[Concept album|conceptual]] storyline with characters, settings, and [[Alter ego|alter egos]] told through several albums. Its live shows feature [[costumes]] and on-stage [[acrobatics]], and the duo has built a famously dedicated [[fanbase]].

The band was formed in 2009 by Joseph, bassist Nick Thomas and drummer Chris Salih. After independently releasing their first album, ''[[Twenty One Pilots (album)|Twenty One Pilots]]'' (2009), Salih was replaced by Dun in May 2011, and Thomas left the following month, with the group continuing as a duo. Following their second independent album, ''[[Regional at Best]]'' (2011), Twenty One Pilots were signed by record label [[Fueled by Ramen]] in 2012. Their label debut ''[[Vessel (Twenty One Pilots album)|Vessel]]'' (2013) became the second album in history on which every track received at least a gold certification.

The duo achieved breakthrough success in 2015 with their fourth album, ''[[Blurryface]]'', which produced the hit singles "[[Stressed Out]]" and "[[Ride (Twenty One Pilots song)|Ride]]" and became the first album on which every track received at least a [[RIAA certification|gold certification]] from the [[Recording Industry Association of America]]. "Stressed Out" earned Twenty One Pilots a [[Grammy Award for Best Pop Duo/Group Performance]] at the [[59th Annual Grammy Awards]]. The release of "[[Heathens (Twenty One Pilots song)|Heathens]]" in 2016 also made the group the first alternative artist in history to have two concurrent top five singles in the [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] and the third rock act in history to have two singles simultaneously chart in the top five of the ''Billboard'' Hot 100, joining [[the Beatles]] and [[Elvis Presley]].

The duo has released four more studio albums: ''[[Trench (album)|Trench]]'' (2018), ''[[Scaled and Icy]]'' (2021), ''[[Clancy (album)|Clancy]]'' (2024), and ''[[Breach (Twenty One Pilots album)|Breach]]'' (2025). They have garnered 14 number-one songs on the [[Alternative Airplay]] chart, tying with [[Linkin Park]] for the second-most.
